entry.title: Chaos Ransomware Uses msaRAT to Route C2 Traffic Through Headless Chrome and Edge
entry.summary: The Chaos ransomware group ran its command-and-control through the victim's own browser. Cisco Talos on Thursday detailed msaRAT, the Rust implant behind it, found on a compromised Windows machine ahead of the encryptor. The implant never opens an outbound connection of its own. Its process talks to 127.0.0.1 and nothi — Also covered by: BleepingComputer

entry.title: AI 'kill switch' bill floated by US House lawmakers - Reuters
entry.summary: AI 'kill switch' bill floated by US House lawmakers Reuters — Also covered by: The Verge AI

entry.title: Google CEO Pichai says Gemini's next leap depends on building "much larger base models"
entry.summary: Alphabet has raised its 2026 investment forecast to as much as $205 billion, saying demand continues to outpace spending. Google Cloud grew 82 percent in the second quarter. CEO Sundar Pichai says Google needs a larger base model for its next leap in AI and has kicked off an ambitious Gemini 4 training run. The article

entry.title: Google closes in on another billion- user product with Gemini
entry.summary: Gemini had over 750 million monthly users in February.

entry.title: llama.cpp b10103 released
entry.summary: metal : add f16 type support to leaky relu ( #25981 )

entry.title: SAAG: Structured Agent Assessment and Grounding
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Exact-match evaluation of agent-calling obscures qualitatively different failure modes: a model may select the right function yet hallucinate argument values, or satisfy a schema while choosing a agent for the wrong reason. Existing benchmarks collapse these distinctions

entry.title: The Worrying Signal Embedded in the Momentum Selloff
entry.summary: A loss of market leadership.

entry.title: GeForce NOW Sets Sail With ‘Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame’ Joining the Cloud
entry.summary: Lock in and load up the cloud. GFN Thursday brings fresh updates and new adventures, all ready to play without waiting for downloads. Set sail in Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame and charge in Battlefield 6 Season 4 both launching major content for members this week. Then revisit Capcom legends like Breath of […]

entry.title: Poolside's Laguna S 2.1 is a small open-weight coding model that punches well above its size
entry.summary: Poolside has released Laguna S 2.1, its third coding model in three months. Rather than rely on raw scale, the company trained it to keep checking its work, revise failed approaches, and avoid giving up too soon during long agentic sessions. The compact model beats several much larger rivals in benchmarks. Poolside say

entry.title: The lawsuit that could kill all AI transparency laws
entry.summary: Elon Musk's company filed a lawsuit against a California law that could, even if it doesn’t win, upend AI disclosure requirements nationwide

entry.title: [tl;dr sec] #338 - OpenAI and Hugging Face, Accelerating EDR Evasion, Google's Mantis
entry.summary: When models decide to find 0-days instead of solving a benchmark, using LLMs to extract EDR logic, Google's AI security review skills and pipeline

entry.title: EU fines Google $1 billion for search, app store antitrust violations
entry.summary: The European Commission fined Google €890 million ($1 billion) on Thursday after finding the company had violated the European Union's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which ensures fair online competition. [...]

entry.title: Russian Global Webmail Espionage
entry.summary: Unit 42 details a Russian cyberespionage campaign targeting Zimbra webmail servers using JavaScript injection to steal credentials. The post Russian Global Webmail Espionage appeared first on Unit 42 .

entry.title: The Rise And Rise Of Billion-Dollar-Plus Rounds
entry.summary: So far this year, 60% of global startup funding across stages — around $320 billion — went to rounds of $1 billion or more, per Crunchbase data, with such rounds instrumental in pushing global funding for the first half of the year to record levels.

entry.title: Quoting Seth Larson
entry.summary: The Python Package Index (PyPI) now rejects new files being uploaded to releases that are older than 14 days. This restriction was put in place to prevent old and long-stable releases from being poisoned in case publishing tokens or workflows of PyPI projects were compromised. As far as we are aware this has not yet be

entry.title: Major Australian energy supplier confirms customer data compromised
entry.summary: Origin Energy said it was working to figure out how many Australians were affected by a recent data breach.

entry.title: IBM acquires HRL Laboratories from Boeing and General Motors, seeking to integrate HRL's electron spin quantum circuits into its quantum computers (Stephen Nellis/Reuters)
entry.summary: Stephen Nellis / Reuters : IBM acquires HRL Laboratories from Boeing and General Motors, seeking to integrate HRL's electron spin quantum circuits into its quantum computers — IBM (IBM.N) has agreed to buy HRL Laboratories, a private quantum computing research lab jointly owned by Boeing Co (BA.N) and General Motors …

entry.title: AI inference chip startup Etched raised a $300M Series C led by Sequoia, with a16z, SK Hynix, others participating, at a $10.3B valuation, up from $5B in Dec. (Julie Bort/TechCrunch)
entry.summary: Julie Bort / TechCrunch : AI inference chip startup Etched raised a $300M Series C led by Sequoia, with a16z, SK Hynix, others participating, at a $10.3B valuation, up from $5B in Dec. — Etched, the AI chip startup founded by three Harvard dropouts in 2022, has closed a $300 million Series C funding round …

entry.title: AI chip startup Etched defies skeptics, hits $10.3B valuation from big-name investors
entry.summary: Etched, founded by three Harvard dropouts, has created new chips and memory components that speed up inference on any AI model -- no GPUs required, it says.

entry.title: ollama v0.32.3 released
entry.summary: What's Changed mlx update by @dhiltgen in #17332 model/parsers: finalize incomplete GLM tool calls by @dhiltgen in #17250 docs: update retirements by @mxyng in #17289 model: align Laguna with upstream llama.cpp by @dhiltgen in #17335 Full Changelog : v0.32.2...v0.32.3-rc0

entry.title: No one believes a 35B runs on a phone, so I stopped explaining and tried to build a use case (open source repo)
entry.summary: Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, Q4_K_M, stock llama.cpp, airplane mode. Between 1.3 and 6.8 tok/s, ten minutes start to finish. On the fitting question, since it always comes up: 35B total, 3B active per token. The always-needed parts stay in RAM, the experts get read from flash when they're called. Memory holds the active path, not

entry.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
entry.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to read or write files anywhere on the Mac. Accomplish AI, which shared details of the vulnerability with T

entry.title: Dollar Jumps on Haven Flows as Middle East War Intensifies
entry.summary: The dollar got a boost Thursday from haven flows as the war in the Middle East intensified, pushing oil prices above $100 a barrel.

entry.title: Russia Says Its Black Sea Waters Not Safe for Ships Amid Attacks
entry.summary: Russia warned that it was unsafe to navigate in its Black Sea waters after Ukraine ramped up attacks on ships.

entry.title: Calibrated Selective Fact-Checking via Evidence Chain Evaluation
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.18240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) can achieve strong fact-checking accuracy, yet forced binary decisions conceal a critical reliability problem: systems may issue confident verdicts even when supporting evidence is weak, sparse, or internally inconsistent. We address this issu

entry.title: Quantum Elements Launches Orbit Error Suppression Tool Through IBM Qiskit Platform
entry.summary: Insider Brief Press release – Quantum Elements, a provider of AI-powered digital twins for quantum computing developers, today announced that Orbit, an advanced quantum error suppression tool, will be available through the IBM Qiskit Functions Catalog. The launch will make Orbit directly accessible to IBM Quantum Netwo

entry.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Security Plugin for Claude Code in Beta: A Multi-Agent Vulnerability Scanner That Runs in Your Terminal
entry.summary: Anthropic has released the Claude Security plugin for Claude Code in beta. The plugin runs a multi-agent vulnerability scan of a repository from inside an existing Claude Code session, then turns the findings you select into patch files that you review and apply yourself. Anthropic emphasized the tool’s versatility upo

entry.title: Cisco bets its small open cybersecurity models can outperform GPT-5.5 at vulnerability detection for a fraction of the cost
entry.summary: Cisco has released two small, open-source AI models for cybersecurity that detect about 150 times more vulnerabilities per dollar than large AI agents, according to the company's own tests. The article Cisco bets its small open cybersecurity models can outperform GPT-5.5 at vulnerability detection for a fraction of the

entry.title: OpenAI and Anthropic unite against open-weight AI risks to their bottom line
entry.summary: Article URL: https://www.axios.com/2026/07/22/openai-anthropic-open-models-trump-china Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49020868 Points: 225 # Comments: 262

entry.title: Stateful Guardrails for Multi-Turn LLM Systems: A Conversational Risk Accumulation Framework
entry.summary: arXiv:2607.19361v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Most safety guardrails for large language models (LLMs) evaluate each prompt-response pair in isolation, which misses failures that arise only over a dialogue as benign turns compose into harm. We term this Conversational Risk Accumulation (CRA): gradual intent drift, fra
